segment_description: Users who both buy and sell on Purple Wave and gave low NPS scores. Dual-role users experiencing friction.
segment_comment_themes: **1. High Buyer's Premium/Fees (13 comments):** Users consistently complain about the 10% buyer's premium being too high, especially on expensive items, with many requesting caps or sliding scales for large purchases. **2. Account Restrictions/Bans (6 comments):** Multiple users report being unexpectedly banned, having accounts halted, or being cut off from bidding without clear explanations or adequate customer service response. **3. Poor Selling Experience/Low Returns (8 comments):** Sellers express frustration with equipment selling below expected ranges, long payment delays, missing listing details, and high listing fees for multiple items. **4. Listing Quality/Transparency Issues (7 comments):** Users cite problems with poor video quality, inaccurate descriptions, missing important details, and sellers not being honest about item conditions. **5. Inconsistent Policies/Selectivity (4 comments):** Complaints about inconsistent application of listing policies and Purple Wave being too selective about what items they'll accept for auction. Overall, these dual-role users feel caught between high costs as buyers and disappointing returns as sellers, leading to a loss of trust in the platform's value proposition.

nps_comment: When you started charging buyers fees as opposed to sellers fees things started to go downhill. Also your description and pictures of the lot are not what they used to be. nps_comment: It depends on buying or selling.  As a seller it’s a great platform that reaches a huge number of buyers so I would highly recommend it.  On the other hand as a buyer I’m very careful due to the 10% buyer premium gets pretty ridiculous on big ticket items.  I feel as the price goes up into the thousands that premium should decrease or even have a cap.  That being said I’m less enthusiastic about recommending it for purchasing.  Thank you.

segment_description: Detractors from the quarter with the highest detractor rate (18.7%). Something changed this quarter.
segment_comment_themes: **Fee Structure Complaints (6 comments)**: Customers are frustrated with the 10% buyer's premium and additional fees, viewing them as uncompetitive compared to other auction sites. **Technical and Operational Issues (6 comments)**: Users report problems with search functionality, payment processing, slow title transfers, lack of mobile app, and bidding difficulties due to technical constraints. **Process and Policy Frustrations (4 comments)**: Detractors are upset about auction time extensions, reopened closed auctions, and lack of seller accountability for misrepresented items. **Dismissive/Hostile Responses (8 comments)**: Many responses are extremely brief or hostile, suggesting high levels of frustration that customers don't want to elaborate on. **New User Experience (4 comments)**: Several detractors indicate they are new users or haven't used the platform recently, suggesting onboarding or re-engagement challenges. The overall takeaway is that this spike quarter saw significant dissatisfaction with core platform economics (fees), technical functionality, and operational processes, particularly among newer users.

segment_description: High-value bidders averaging over $50k per bid who gave low scores. These represent the highest-dollar customers.
segment_comment_themes: **Tax and Legal Compliance Issues** (1 comment): Serious operational failures in tax law application that required customer dispute resolution and external verification. **High Buyer Fees** (2 comments): Premium customers find the fee structure too expensive and actively factor these costs into their bidding strategies. **Poor Platform Usability** (2 comments): Search functionality, filtering systems, and overall navigation are inferior compared to competing auction platforms. **Pricing Concerns** (1 comment): Equipment selling below expected market value from a seller's perspective. The overarching takeaway is that high-value customers are experiencing fundamental operational and platform deficiencies that directly impact their willingness to continue using the service.

segment_comment_themes: **Tax and Legal Compliance Issues** (1 comment): Serious operational failures in tax law application that required customer dispute resolution and external verification. **High Buyer Fees** (2 comments): Premium customers find the fee structure too expensive and actively factor these costs into their bidding strategies. **Poor Platform Usability** (2 comments): Search functionality, filtering systems, and overall navigation are inferior compared to competing auction platforms. **Pricing Concerns** (1 comment): Equipment selling below expected market value from a seller's perspective. The overarching takeaway is that high-value customers are experiencing fundamental operational and platform deficiencies that directly impact their willingness to continue using the service.

segment_name: Lapsed Bidder Detractors (bid over 1 year ago)
segment_description: Users who haven't bid in over a year and gave low scores. Potentially lost customers.
segment_comment_themes: **Buyer's Premium Complaints (8 comments)**: Users consistently cite the 10% buyer's premium as uncompetitive and a primary reason for choosing other platforms. **Trust and Transparency Issues (7 comments)**: Customers express concerns about shill bidding, poor item descriptions, and sellers misrepresenting equipment condition, creating distrust in the platform's integrity. **Poor Customer Experience (6 comments)**: Users report receiving defective items, unfulfilled transactions, account bans without explanation, and inadequate customer service response. **Operational Difficulties (5 comments)**: Complaints about website navigation, auction access barriers, high shipping costs, and geographic limitations in the Midwest. **Negative Past Experiences (4 comments)**: General references to "bad experiences" that have permanently damaged their relationship with the platform. The overarching takeaway is that these lapsed bidders have fundamental trust and value concerns that have driven them to competitors, with financial structure and transparency being the primary barriers to their return.

segment_comment_themes: **Buyer's Premium Complaints (8 comments)**: Users consistently cite the 10% buyer's premium as uncompetitive and a primary reason for choosing other platforms. **Trust and Transparency Issues (7 comments)**: Customers express concerns about shill bidding, poor item descriptions, and sellers misrepresenting equipment condition, creating distrust in the platform's integrity. **Poor Customer Experience (6 comments)**: Users report receiving defective items, unfulfilled transactions, account bans without explanation, and inadequate customer service response. **Operational Difficulties (5 comments)**: Complaints about website navigation, auction access barriers, high shipping costs, and geographic limitations in the Midwest. **Negative Past Experiences (4 comments)**: General references to "bad experiences" that have permanently damaged their relationship with the platform. The overarching takeaway is that these lapsed bidders have fundamental trust and value concerns that have driven them to competitors, with financial structure and transparency being the primary barriers to their return.

nps_comment:
The problem with most of the items I look at on your site is lack of a good description and details about the item. This is a problem with most online auctions in my opinion. Most people don't have the time to actually go and inspect every piece of equipment or vehicle they have an interest in so they rely on the pictures and information provided.
 I also don't like the 10% buyers premium you use. Sell it and take your commission out  of what it brings instead of hoping people will forget about the premium while bidding.

segment_comment_themes: **Buyer's Premium Backlash (25-30 comments)**: The 10% buyer's premium with no cap is the most frequently cited complaint, with veterans calling it "too high," "expensive," and "cost prohibitive" especially on large equipment purchases. **Quality and Disclosure Issues (20-25 comments)**: Experienced users report receiving misrepresented equipment, undisclosed defects, and items in much worse condition than described, with some calling purchases "junk" or "pieces of crap." **Operational and Service Problems (15-20 comments)**: Veterans cite account suspensions without explanation, slow payment processing, poor customer service, website navigation difficulties, and inadequate item descriptions. **Fee Structure Complaints (10-15 comments)**: Beyond the buyer's premium, users complain about high listing fees, commission rates, and overall cost structure compared to competitors. **Trust and Bidding Concerns (8-10 comments)**: Some veterans suspect shill bidding, dishonest practices, and artificial price inflation. These long-tenured users' feedback reflects deep frustration with platform changes over time, particularly the shift to buyer-paid fees and perceived decline in service quality since corporate changes.

nps_comment:
The problem with most of the items I look at on your site is lack of a good description and details about the item. This is a problem with most online auctions in my opinion. Most people don't have the time to actually go and inspect every piece of equipment or vehicle they have an interest in so they rely on the pictures and information provided.

These long-tenured users' feedback reflects deep frustration with platform changes over time, particularly the shift to buyer-paid fees and perceived decline in service quality since corporate changes.

nps_comment: When you started charging buyers fees as opposed to sellers fees things started to go downhill. Also your description and pictures of the lot are not what they used to be. One other thing and it is not just Purple Wave, "Condition unknown." Sometimes that may apply but the majority of the time it means something is being covered up. Since the above changes I do not do nearly as much business with Purple Wave as I once did . Mike WIlborn

nps_comment: It depends on buying or selling.  As a seller it’s a great platform that reaches a huge number of buyers so I would highly recommend it.  On the other hand as a buyer I’m very careful due to the 10% buyer premium gets pretty ridiculous on big ticket items.  I feel as the price goes up into the thousands that premium should decrease or even have a cap.  That being said I’m less enthusiastic about recommending it for purchasing.  Thank you.

nps_comment: I have been pleased with most purchases until the last one that we purchased a 2007 Peterbilt from Collinsville OK.  The auction yard left it out of the because I could not get there  by closing time, which was nice.  However, the truck batteries were completely dead, so dead that 2 sets of jumper cables would not start it, it still had enough air pressure that I was able to have my wife pull me with my pickup to get it started.  Then the clutch was so still that I had to put my foot under the pedal to pull it up to engage it.  I don' mind those things but am very troubled by the fact that it was not disclosed and am disappointed in Purple Wave for that.  Always in past experiences, Purple wave has be very accurate with inspections and reporting flaws.  I know that the batteries did not just die that dead in one day and the clutch did not get that stiff in one day.  I was able to get it home but my wife had to pull me to start it 3 times.
